title: PE foundations
summary: Introduces the abstract bulk-boundary data and the formal metric-expression language used by the Poincaré–Einstein layer.

mathematicalContent.heading: Bulk and conformal infinity
mathematicalContent.body: The module represents a compactified bulk by a type X̄ with boundary M, with dim X̄ = n + 1 and dim M = n. It records only the boundary inclusion and the set of interior points; no topology or smooth structure has yet been installed.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal metric symbols
mathematicalContent.body: MetricSymbol is syntax for expressions built from zero, atomic symbols, addition, scalar multiplication, and multiplication by a real-valued function. Therefore g₊ and ricci below are formal symbols, not tensors computed by a differential-geometric backend.
mathematicalContent.formula: \mathscr M(X)\;::=\;0\mid g_k\mid g+h\mid a g\mid f g

mathematicalContent.heading: Einstein right-hand side
mathematicalContent.body: If the conformal boundary has dimension n, the expression used later for the Poincaré–Einstein equation is −n g₊. This file defines that right-hand side but does not assert that the stored Ricci symbol equals it.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Packages a compactified bulk X̄, its boundary M, dimensions n+1 and n, an inclusion ι : M → X̄, an interior subset X°, and the assertion that boundary points are not interior points.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \left(\overline X,M,n+1,n,\iota,X^{\circ}\right),\qquad \dim\overline X=\dim M+1,\quad \forall x\in M,\;\iota(x)\notin X^{\circ}
correspondence.leanType: structure PESpace where bulk; boundary; bulkDim; boundaryDim; dim_relation; boundaryInclusion; interior; boundary_not_interior

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Restates the dimension relation stored in a PESpace: the bulk has exactly one dimension more than its conformal boundary.
Defines a scalar-function symbol on X simply as a real-valued function on the underlying type.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev FunctionSymbol (X : Type u) := X -> Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the free syntax of formal metric expressions: zero, numbered atoms, sums, real scalar multiples, and multiples by scalar functions.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \mathscr M(X)::=0\mid g_k\mid g_1+g_2\mid a g\mid f g,\qquad k\in\mathbb N,\ a\in\mathbb R,\ f:X\to\mathbb R
correspondence.leanType: inductive MetricSymbol (X : Type u) | zero | atom | add | scalarSMul | functionSMul

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Builds the formal product of a scalar function a(x) and a metric expression g.
correspondence.leanType: def scaleByFunction (a : FunctionSymbol X) (g : MetricSymbol X) : MetricSymbol X

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Builds the formal product of a real scalar a and a metric expression g.
correspondence.leanType: def scaleByScalar (a : Real) (g : MetricSymbol X) : MetricSymbol X

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Chooses a conformal structure [h] on the boundary M of the compactified bulk.
correspondence.leanType: structure ConformalInfinity (X : PESpace) where boundaryConf : ConformalStructure X.boundary

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores the conformal infinity [h], a formal bulk metric g₊, and a separate formal symbol intended to represent Ric(g₊). No relation between the last two fields is imposed here.
correspondence.leanType: structure PEMetricData (X : PESpace) where conformalInfinity; gPlus; ricci

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For boundary dimension n, defines the formal right-hand side of the negative Einstein equation to be −n times g₊.
correspondence.leanType: def einsteinRHS (X : PESpace) (gPlus : MetricSymbol X.bulk) : MetricSymbol X.bulk

references.note: Background for conformal infinity and Poincaré metrics; this Lean file contains only the abstract carrier and symbolic-expression layer.

limitations: This is scaffold code. PESpace has no manifold, topology, boundary regularity, or tensor calculus, and MetricSymbol is only syntax. In particular, PEMetricData does not calculate a Ricci tensor or prove the Einstein equation.
